description: High-altitude scan of ZDTP convergence near n=5000 Riemann zero. Tests the transparency hypothesis where structural stability increases at higher zero heights.

transparency_band_scan.description: ZDTP convergence score at sigma=0.5 for zeros near n=5000.

experiment_5_regime_detection_sequence.description: 12-point ZDTP convergence sequence for regime detection analysis (sigma 0.1 to 2.0).

altitude_comparison.description: Stability at n=5000 is higher than at the first zero (gamma_1), confirming the transparency band effect.

interpretation: A localized peak in structural stability occurs at n=5000. This validates the Transparency Band hypothesis: as zero height increases, the sedenion encoding of the Euler product becomes increasingly 'transparent' to the underlying algebraic structure, reducing noise from the prime distribution gaps.
phase_70_relevance: Supports Route 1 by demonstrating that analytic continuation becomes computationally more stable at high altitudes. Correlates with the HWHM scan requirement in Tier 2.
